commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1553443a38f0f689e94d8b50b4b929ce0c32f677)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Anthropic usage auth to suppress usage fallback for standard API keys while preserving OAuth/setup-token usage auth and updates provider-usage auth tests.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +28. Total +63 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: on current main, an Anthropic API-key-only setup misses OAuth usage auth and then falls back to the generic API-key usage auth path. I did not run a live Anthropic status command in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer API/compatibility direction for the usage-auth no-fallback contract plus contributor proof of the real status path, not a narrow safe automation repair.

target.title: fix #85124: skip Anthropic API keys for usage status
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Anthropic standard API-key profiles were eligible for provider usage fallback, so status usage could send them to the OAuth usage endpoint and show a misleading invalid bearer-token error.
- Solution: Keep standard Anthropic API keys out of provider-owned usage auth while preserving setup-token usage auth and other providers' API-key fallback behavior.
- What changed: Updated the Anthropic provider usage auth hook, provider usage fallback handling, and the provider usage auth regression tests.
- What did NOT change: Inference auth, OAuth/setup-token usage auth, and API-key usage fallback for non-Anthropic providers.

Fixes #85124

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw status --usage` no longer treats a standard Anthropic API key as a usage token for the OAuth usage endpoint.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v24.16.0, OpenClaw v2026.5.22, worktree SHA 1553443.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  PATH=/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-85666 OPENCLAW_LOG=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-85666-evidence/dev-gateway-script-19002.log OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=mytoken123 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19002 bash /media/vdc/code/ai/Hermes/scripts/dev-gateway-start.sh
  browser-use --session pr85666 --headed open http://127.0.0.1:19002/
  browser-use --session pr85666 input 4 ws://127.0.0.1:19002
  browser-use --session pr85666 input 2 mytoken123
  browser-use --session pr85666 click 20
  browser-use --session pr85666 type "请只回复 pong，用于本地启动验证。"
  browser-use --session pr85666 click 853
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  $ bash scripts/dev-gateway-start.sh with OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19002
  [gateway] loading configuration…
  [gateway] resolving authentication…
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
  - openai/deepseek-chat model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] starting HTTP server...
  [gateway] agent model: openai/deepseek-chat (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, openai, phone-control, talk-voice; 3.8s)
  [gateway] ready

  $ browser-use --headed open http://127.0.0.1:19002/
  OpenClaw Control UI connected after setting WebSocket URL ws://127.0.0.1:19002 and token.
  Chat model selector showed DeepSeek Chat.
  Sent: 请只回复 pong，用于本地启动验证。
  Assistant: pong

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  27 passed (27)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The current PR head started a real OpenClaw gateway on port 19002 with token auth and DeepSeek configured, the headed browser connected to the Control UI, and a real chat message returned `pong`; the Anthropic usage-auth regression tests passed on the same head.
- **What was not tested:** no known gaps for the patched API-key usage path

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts`, `src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Standard Anthropic API keys no longer resolve as provider usage auth, while Anthropic setup tokens from API-key sources remain eligible.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the auth boundary before any unsupported usage endpoint request can be made.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The generic provider usage fallback reused API-key credentials after Anthropic's OAuth-only usage hook returned no token, so standard API keys could be passed to the OAuth usage fetcher.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Tests covered OAuth profile ordering but not the API-key-only Anthropic status usage path.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
